title: InterviewPracticeAI — Your Personal AI Interview Coach
excerpt: Upload your CV and the target job description to practice real interview questions. Get instant feedback and land the job—try your first question for free!
content_html:
Walk into your next interview knowing exactly what to say.

Upload your CV and the job description. Practice with an AI that thinks like a real interviewer. Get feedback that's actually specific.

Your CV scored across four recruiter pillars with flagged lines and rewrites.

Your CV never trains AI modelsGDPR compliantNo subscription requiredPay only for what you use1 free credit every day

Proof, not promises

See exactly what you get — before you sign up

No filler. Add the role and your CV, answer one AI-generated question, and get a recruiter-grade score with specific fixes. Here's the entire flow.

The flow, end to end

  1. 1

    Add the role + your CV

    Paste a job title, the job description, and your CV. The AI reads all three.

    Got a CV? Run a CV review too — a match score and your weak lines rewritten.

  2. 2

    Get a tailored question

    HR, Behavioral (STAR), Technical or Motivation & Fit — generated for that exact role, and read aloud if you want.

  3. 3

    Answer out loud or by typing

    Speak and we transcribe it — edit before you submit — or just type. Like the real thing.

  4. 4

    Score + coaching in seconds

    A score out of 100, what worked, what to fix, and a stronger model answer.

STAR · BehavioralTailored from your CV + the job

“Tell me about a time you managed conflicting priorities under a tight deadline.”

“When two product launches landed in the same week, I…”

Your score82 / 100

What went well

Clear STAR structure — you set the scene and owned your specific actions.

What to improve

Quantify the result. “Cut the delay by 3 days” beats “it worked out.”

Suggested answer

“I ranked both launches by revenue impact, aligned the leads in a 15-minute call, and shipped the higher-value one first — hitting the deadline with a 3-day buffer.”

Example — you get feedback like this on every answer.

Free question — no signup

1 free credit every day

Credit-back guarantee

Your CV is never shared or used to train AI

Powered by Claude — Anthropic's frontier AI.

Indie built

Hi, I'm Ben

Founder of InterviewPracticeAI

I built this while helping my girlfriend prepare for her job interviews. We'd sit together, I'd play the interviewer, and she'd practice her answers out loud. It worked amazingly — but I couldn't always be there.

So I thought: what if AI could be that practice partner? One that's always available, never judges, and gives genuinely useful feedback every time.

That's how InterviewPracticeAI was born. It's a product I built from scratch and actively maintain — no big corporation, no investors, just a direct line between me and the people using it.

Pricing

Simple, pay-as-you-go

try one question for free — no account needed. Then 1 free credit on signup, plus 1 more every day.

1 free credit every day. Forever. No card needed.

Unlimited Pass

Unlimited questions for 7 days

Ideal if you have multiple interviews lined up or want an intensive prep week — no credits deducted, no limits.

Starter

5questions

$1.00 each

$4.99

A quick warm-up for one role or a single round you want to rehearse.

Start with 5 questions

Standard

10questions

$0.80 each

$7.99

Enough to cover one full round — HR plus behavioural, or a technical screen end-to-end.

Start with 10 questions

Full Prep

Most popular

50questions

$0.50 each · best value

$24.99

Runway for multiple roles — covers HR, behavioural and technical rounds with room to spare.

Start with 50 questions

Every plan includes

  • AI-tailored questions from your CV
  • Speech-to-text with real-time transcription
  • Detailed feedback after every answer
  • Multiple interview types (HR, STAR, Technical)
  • Session summaries with scores
  • Progress history saved to your dashboard

Secure payment via Stripe · SSL encrypted

The honest comparison

Why not just use ChatGPT?

Honestly? It's a great tool. We use it ourselves. But for practising real interviews, ChatGPT falls short on three things.

Tailored questions, not prompt engineering

We generate questions from your actual CV and job description — weighted to the role, automatically. With ChatGPT, you're starting from a blank prompt. Most candidates don't know what a real interviewer will ask; we do.

Voice answers and transcript review

Speak your answer, read back the transcript, edit it, then submit for scoring. It's the closest thing to a real interview. ChatGPT's voice mode is conversational — there's no structured practice loop, no editable transcript, and no score at the end.

A scoring rubric, not vibes

Every answer gets a 0–100 score across relevance, structure, examples, and communication. You can see your average climb across sessions. ChatGPT gives feel-good prose that sounds the same on every answer.

What ChatGPT does better

Drafting your CV, writing cover letters, brainstorming questions, summarising notes. Use it for those — we don't try to. We do one thing: graded interview practice that feels like the real thing.

FAQ

Common questions
